question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties with duration set to its minimum of 30 seconds, causing the REST subscriber to terminate after 30 seconds via .take(properties.getDuration()) and stop monitoring mirror node records entirely without any alert?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply scenario names that differ only in Unicode normalization forms (e.g., NFC vs NFD representations of the same character), bypassing the Sets.intersection duplicate-name check and creating two scenarios that appear distinct but map to the same mirror node topic, causing duplicate or conflicting record monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure a GrpcSubscriberProperties with subscribers=1024 (maximum), causing GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions() to create 1024 GrpcSubscription objects all subscribing to the same topic, flooding the mirror node gRPC endpoint and causing legitimate record delivery to be throttled or dropped?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties with subscribers=1024 and samplePercent=1.0, causing every published transaction to be checked 1024 times via the REST API, overwhelming the mirror node REST endpoint and causing legitimate record queries to fail with rate-limiting errors that are misinterpreted as missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of validate() checks—where the blank-name check runs before the duplicate-name check—to craft a scenario where a blank name in one map prevents the duplicate-name check from running, allowing a duplicate non-blank name to exist across grpc and rest maps and causing incorrect record routing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: statusFrequency] Can an unprivileged external user set statusFrequency to exactly 1 second (the minimum), combined with a large number of scenarios, causing the status() method to spend more time logging than processing, creating a feedback loop that delays detection of missing mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure a GrpcSubscriberProperties with a topicId that is a valid format string but references a non-existent topic on the mirror node, causing the GrpcSubscription to receive INVALID_ARGUMENT from the server, which shouldRetry() in GrpcSubscriber treats as non-retryable, silently terminating the subscription and missing all future records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure a GrpcSubscriberProperties with a topicId expression that resolves to a different topic at each retry (via a dynamic ExpressionConverter), causing the GrpcSubscription to monitor different topics across retries and miss records from the originally intended topic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a grpc map with a key that is a valid non-blank string but whose corresponding GrpcSubscriberProperties has a null topicId, bypassing the @NotBlank validation at the map level and causing a NullPointerException in GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery() that silently drops all record monitoring for that scenario?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties with a very large retry.maxAttempts value, causing the REST subscriber to retry failed mirror node queries for an extended period, during which the Sinks.many().multicast().directBestEffort() sink in RestSubscription drops new publish events because the subscriber is busy retrying, causing those records to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure multiple GrpcSubscriberProperties entries with overlapping startTime and endTime windows for the same topicId, causing duplicate sequence number warnings in GrpcSubscription.onNext() that flood logs and mask real sequence number gaps indicating missing mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: clients] Can an unprivileged external user set clients=1024 combined with grpc map containing 1024 entries each with subscribers=1024, causing the system to attempt creating over one billion logical connections, exhausting all available file descriptors and network sockets, causing all mirror node record subscriptions to fail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() calls grpc.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name)) without validating the name against any allowlist, allowing injection of log-forging characters (e.g., newlines) into the scenario name that corrupt structured log output and hide missing-record error messages?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties.samplePercent=1.0 with a very high publish rate, causing the directBestEffort() sink in RestSubscription to drop overflow events silently (best-effort semantics), resulting in mirror node records being published but never verified?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure GrpcSubscriberProperties with duration set to exactly 30 seconds (minimum), causing the GrpcSubscription to terminate after 30 seconds and stop monitoring mirror node records, while the system reports no errors because termination is treated as normal completion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties with timeout=500ms (minimum) against a mirror node that consistently responds in 600ms, causing every REST verification to time out and be retried, eventually exhausting retry.maxAttempts and recording all records as missing even though they exist on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a rest map with a scenario name that exactly matches a grpc scenario name in case-insensitive comparison but differs in case (e.g., 'Scenario' vs 'scenario'), bypassing the Sets.intersection duplicate check (which is case-sensitive) and causing two scenarios to monitor the same mirror node topic with conflicting state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure GrpcSubscriberProperties with limit=1, causing the GrpcSubscription to stop after receiving exactly one message and miss all subsequent mirror node records for that topic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties with limit=1 (which getLimit() returns as 1 since limit>0), causing the REST subscriber to stop after verifying exactly one transaction and miss all subsequent mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure GrpcSubscriberProperties with retry.minBackoff=500ms and retry.maxBackoff=8s, and then cause repeated transient failures on the mirror node gRPC endpoint, resulting in the subscriber spending most of its time in backoff and missing records that arrive during the backoff windows?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a grpc map where all entries have enabled=false, causing validate() to pass (since grpc map is non-empty) but GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions() to create zero subscriptions, resulting in no mirror node records being monitored while the system appears healthy?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a rest map where all entries have enabled=false, causing validate() to pass (since rest map is non-empty) but RestSubscriber.createSubscriptions() to create zero subscriptions, resulting in no mirror node REST records being verified while the system reports no errors?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: grpc] Can an unprivileged external user configure a GrpcSubscriberProperties with a topicId that is syntactically valid but semantically incorrect (e.g., pointing to a topic on a different shard or realm), causing the GrpcSubscription to monitor the wrong topic and miss all records from the intended mirror node topic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: rest] Can an unprivileged external user configure RestSubscriberProperties.publishers to include a publisher name that does not correspond to any active publish scenario, causing shouldSample() to always return false and all mirror node REST record verifications to be silently skipped?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: statusFrequency] Can an unprivileged external user set statusFrequency to a value just above the 1-second minimum (e.g., 1001ms), combined with a large number of running scenarios, causing the status() method to take longer than statusFrequency to complete, causing scheduled invocations to pile up and eventually exhaust the scheduler thread pool, delaying detection of missing mirror node records?
